Our story

Built on Passion.
Driven by Quality.

We're a proudly local business dedicated to bringing you exceptional products at honest prices — with service that actually feels personal.

Who we are

A Business Built Around You

FMD Electrical (Pty) Ltd was founded with a single purpose: to deliver professional, reliable, and fully compliant electrical services to homes, businesses, and industries across Gauteng and beyond.

We carefully curate every product in our catalogue — testing, sourcing, and selecting only items we would proudly recommend to friends and family. No filler. No fluff. Just goods worth buying.

We're not a faceless warehouse. We're a small team that genuinely cares about your experience — from the moment you browse our store to the day your order arrives at your door.

What drives us

Our Mission & Vision

We exist to make quality accessible — removing the barriers between great products and the people who deserve them.

Our Mission

To provide every customer with a seamless, trustworthy shopping experience — offering products that deliver real value and service that goes the extra mile.

Our Vision

To become the most trusted name in local online retail — a store where customers return not just for the products, but because they know they'll be treated right.

Our Promise

Every product is quality-checked. Every enquiry is answered. Every customer matters. That's not a tagline — it's how we've operated since day one.

What we stand for

Our Core Values

Transparency No hidden fees, no misleading descriptions. What you see is what you get — honest pricing and clear product information, always.
Quality First We don't stock products we wouldn't use ourselves. Every item passes our internal quality check before it earns a place in our catalogue.
Customer Care Real people, real responses. We answer every message, solve every problem, and follow up until you're genuinely satisfied.
Community We're proudly local. We support local suppliers, local couriers, and put money back into the communities we serve.
Reliability We follow through. Orders ship on time, enquiries are responded to within 24 hours, and we stand behind every sale we make.
Continuous Improvement We actively seek feedback and use it to grow. If something isn't working, we fix it. No ego, just progress.

Legal & information

Our Policies

Everything you need to know about how we handle your data, orders, returns and deliveries. Click any section to expand.

Sections

Privacy Policy Terms & Conditions Refund Policy Delivery Info
Privacy Policy
Last updated: May 2026
Effective: 1 May 2026

1. Information We Collect

When you place an order enquiry or contact us, we collect personal information you voluntarily provide, including:

  • Full name and contact details (phone number and email address)
  • Delivery address for order fulfilment purposes
  • Product preferences and order details
  • Any additional notes or communications you send us

2. How We Use Your Information

We use your personal information solely to process and fulfil your order enquiries, contact you regarding your order, and improve our service. We do not sell, rent, or share your personal data with third parties for marketing purposes.

Your data is yours. We only store information for as long as necessary to fulfil your order and comply with legal obligations.

3. Data Security

We take reasonable precautions to protect your information. Our website uses secure connections and we limit internal access to your data to staff directly involved in processing your order.

4. Cookies

This website does not currently use tracking cookies or advertising pixels. We may use essential session data to remember your preferences during your visit.

5. Your Rights

You have the right to request access to, correction of, or deletion of your personal data at any time. Contact us at admin@fmdelectrical.co.za to exercise these rights.

6. Contact

For any privacy-related concerns, please contact us at admin@fmdelectrical.co.za. We aim to respond within 5 business days.

Terms & Conditions
Last updated: May 2026
Effective: 1 May 2026

1. Acceptance of Terms

By using FMD Electrical's website and submitting a service enquiry, you agree to be bound by these Terms and Conditions. If you do not agree, please do not use our service.

2. Order Enquiries

Submitting an order enquiry is not a binding purchase. It is a request to confirm availability and pricing. A sale is only confirmed once we have verified stock, confirmed pricing, and received your payment.

  • All prices displayed are in South African Rand (ZAR) and include VAT unless stated otherwise
  • Quoted prices are valid for 30 days from the date of issue
  • We reserve the right to decline any service request at our discretion

3. Product Availability

Product images and descriptions are provided for illustration purposes. We make every effort to ensure accuracy, but we cannot guarantee that product images perfectly represent the final item. Stock availability is subject to change.

4. Intellectual Property

All content on this website — including text, images, logos and design — is the property of FMD Electrical (Pty) Ltd. You may not reproduce or distribute any part of this site without prior written permission.

5. Limitation of Liability

FMD Electrical (Pty) Ltd shall not be held liable for any indirect or consequential loss arising from use of this website, beyond the value of the service contract in question.

6. Governing Law

These terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of South Africa. Any disputes shall be resolved in South African courts.

Questions about our terms? Email admin@fmdelectrical.co.za and we'll be happy to clarify anything.
Warranty & Service Guarantee
Last updated: May 2026
Effective: 1 May 2026
Our commitment: If you're not happy with your order, we'll make it right. Your satisfaction is non-negotiable.

1. Return Window

You may return any item within 7 calendar days of receiving your order, provided it meets the conditions below.

2. Return Conditions

To qualify for a return or exchange, items must be:

  • In their original, unused condition
  • In original packaging with all tags attached
  • Accompanied by proof of purchase (order reference number)
  • Not classified as a non-returnable item (see below)

3. Non-Returnable Items

The following cannot be returned unless faulty:

  • Personalised or custom-made products
  • Opened beauty and hygiene products
  • Digital goods or downloadable products
  • Perishable items

4. How to Request a Return

Contact us via WhatsApp or email at admin@fmdelectrical.co.za with your order reference and reason for return. We'll respond within 2 business days with return instructions.

5. Refund Process

Once your return is received and inspected, we'll notify you of approval. Approved refunds are processed within 5–7 business days via the original payment method.

6. Damaged or Incorrect Items

If you receive a damaged or incorrect product, please contact us within 48 hours of delivery with photographs. We will arrange a replacement or full refund at no additional cost to you.

Project Delivery
Last updated: May 2026
Effective: 1 May 2026

1. Delivery Options

We currently offer the following delivery methods:

  • Standard Delivery: 3–5 business days — R75 flat rate
  • Express Delivery: 1–2 business days — R150 flat rate
  • Click & Collect: Free — available by appointment only
  • Free Standard Delivery: On all orders over R800
Free shipping applies automatically on orders above R800 and will be reflected during order confirmation.

2. Processing Time

Orders are processed within 1–2 business days after payment is confirmed. You will receive a notification with a tracking number once your order is dispatched.

3. Delivery Areas

We currently deliver to all major centres across South Africa. Remote area surcharges may apply for outlying or rural addresses — we will communicate this before confirming your order.

4. Delivery Partners

We work with trusted national courier services to ensure your order arrives safely and on time. Specific courier details will be shared at time of dispatch.

5. Tracking Your Order

Once dispatched, you'll receive a tracking number via email or WhatsApp. You can use this to monitor your delivery in real time through our courier partner's platform.

6. Failed Deliveries

If a delivery attempt fails due to no one being available, our courier will leave a notification card. A re-delivery fee may apply for subsequent attempts. Please ensure someone is available to receive your order at the specified address.

7. International Shipping

We do not currently offer international shipping. Deliveries are limited to addresses within South Africa. Please contact us if you have specific enquiries about cross-border orders.

Insights & advice

FMD Electrical Blog

Expert advice on electrical safety, solar energy, and security solutions from our licensed team in Gauteng.

Solar 5 min read
June 2025
FMD Electrical Team

The Benefits of Solar Power for South African Homes

South Africa's ongoing load shedding crisis has pushed solar power from a luxury to a necessity for thousands of homeowners. Here's why making the switch is one of the smartest investments you can make for your property.

Escaping the Load Shedding Cycle

South Africa has experienced some of the world's most severe load shedding in recent years, with rolling blackouts lasting up to 12 hours a day at their peak. For families and small businesses, this translates to lost productivity, spoiled food, damaged appliances, and significant financial strain. A correctly sized solar and battery backup system can eliminate your dependence on Eskom entirely during daylight hours — and keep your essentials running through the night.

Energy Independence

True energy independence means your home generates and stores its own power. A grid-tied solar system feeds surplus energy back and reduces your municipal bill dramatically, while an off-grid setup with battery storage means you're completely self-sufficient. With lithium battery technology improving every year, homes can now store enough energy to power lights, appliances, and security systems for 24 hours or more without grid input.

Significant Electricity Savings

Eskom tariffs have increased by over 400% in the past decade, and further increases are all but certain. A well-designed 5kW solar system can reduce a typical household electricity bill by 60–80% annually. With an average South African household spending R1,500–R3,000 per month on electricity, the return on investment for a solar installation can be achieved within 4–6 years — after which the power is essentially free.

Did you know? South Africa receives an average of 4.5–6.5 peak sun hours per day — one of the highest solar irradiance levels in the world. This makes SA homes exceptionally well-suited for solar power generation.

Environmental Benefits

South Africa's national grid is still overwhelmingly coal-powered, making it one of the most carbon-intensive grids in the world. By generating clean solar energy, each household installation makes a measurable reduction in carbon emissions. A typical 5kW system prevents approximately 4–6 tonnes of CO₂ from entering the atmosphere each year — equivalent to planting over 150 trees annually.

Battery Backup Systems

Pairing solar panels with a quality battery storage system is the key to full energy independence. Modern lithium-iron-phosphate (LiFePO4) batteries are safe, long-lasting (8,000+ charge cycles), and capable of delivering consistent power output. FMD Electrical designs and installs complete solar-plus-storage solutions — from compact home backup units to large commercial energy storage systems — all professionally installed and COC-certified.

Why Homeowners Are Making the Switch Now

The combination of falling solar panel prices, improved battery technology, rising electricity tariffs, and persistent load shedding has created a perfect environment for rapid solar adoption. Property developers are now including solar as standard, and banks offer green financing products for solar installations. Additionally, Section 12B of the Income Tax Act allows South African taxpayers to claim solar energy investments as a tax deduction — making the financial case even stronger.

Ready to go solar?

Security 6 min read
May 2025
FMD Electrical Team

How CCTV and Security Systems Protect Your Property

Property crime remains one of the most significant concerns for South African homeowners and businesses. A modern, professionally installed security system is no longer a luxury — it's an essential layer of protection that deters criminals and provides peace of mind around the clock.

Understanding Modern Security Threats

Opportunistic criminals actively scout properties before targeting them. Visible security infrastructure — cameras, electric fencing, alarm systems — is often enough to make a criminal move on to an easier target. Statistics consistently show that properties with clearly visible security measures are significantly less likely to be burglarised than those without. The mere presence of a CCTV camera at an entry point can reduce break-in attempts by up to 60%.

CCTV Monitoring: Eyes on Your Property 24/7

Modern IP-based CCTV cameras deliver crystal-clear HD and 4K footage that holds up as evidence in legal proceedings. Intelligent features like motion detection, night vision, and wide-angle lenses mean there are no blind spots and no lapses in coverage. FMD Electrical installs complete CCTV systems — from the camera placement and cabling through to the DVR/NVR configuration — ensuring every critical area of your property is covered and footage is stored securely for 30+ days.

Remote Viewing — With a properly configured IP camera system, you can view live and recorded footage from anywhere in the world using your smartphone. This means you're always connected to your home or business, whether you're at the office or on holiday.

Alarm Systems: Instant Alert, Instant Response

A professionally installed alarm system creates multiple layers of detection. Passive infrared (PIR) motion detectors trigger the moment an intruder enters a space. Door and window contacts alert the system the instant a point of entry is breached. A loud siren draws immediate attention, while an armed response notification is sent to your security company within seconds. This rapid response chain is what separates a professional installation from a DIY approach.

Electric Fencing: The First Line of Defence

An electric fence serves two functions: it delivers a safe but memorable shock to deter would-be intruders, and it raises an alarm the moment the fence is tampered with or cut. SANS 10222-compliant electric fence installations use registered energisers and correctly spaced conductors to ensure maximum deterrence without posing a danger to children or animals. When integrated with your alarm system, any fence breach immediately triggers your full security response.

Access Control: Knowing Who Enters Your Property

Modern access control goes far beyond a standard gate remote. Intercom systems with video allow you to visually verify visitors before granting entry. Biometric access (fingerprint or facial recognition) eliminates the risk of lost keys or copied remote controls. For commercial properties, access control logs every entry and exit event with a time stamp — creating a complete audit trail of who was on site and when.

Protecting Homes and Businesses Alike

Whether you own a suburban home, a rental property, a retail store, or a warehouse, FMD Electrical designs security systems to match your specific risk profile and budget. We conduct a site assessment, identify vulnerabilities, and recommend a layered security approach — combining CCTV, alarms, electric fencing, and access control into a single, integrated system that can be monitored and controlled from one interface.

Protect your property

Request a Security Consultation

Our work

Projects

A showcase of installations completed by FMD Electrical across Gauteng and beyond. These are part of our projects — each one representing our commitment to quality, safety, and lasting results.

From residential rooftop solar to large-scale commercial hybrid energy systems — every project is delivered by our licensed team with full COC certification.

Projects Completed

Years Experience

COC Certified

Solar Vereeniging Security Estate

01.Residential Solar Rooftop Installation

The Brief

A homeowner in a Vereeniging security estate needed reliable solar backup to combat escalating load shedding. The property required a system capable of running essential loads overnight without grid dependency.

The Challenge

Working on a tiled pitched roof of a double-storey property in an occupied estate required careful access management, roof protection, and neat cable routing to meet COC standards and the estate's aesthetic requirements.

The Win

FMD delivered a fully commissioned rooftop solar system — panels mounted, inverter integrated, and COC issued on the same day. The homeowner now runs independently through all load shedding stages with zero interruption.

Start a Similar Project
Solar Large Retail / Commercial Complex, Gauteng

02.Commercial Solar Carport Structure

The Brief

A commercial property required a large-scale solar solution that would generate significant renewable energy without occupying building roof space. The client wanted a dual-purpose structure — covered parking plus power generation.

The Challenge

Engineering a full solar canopy over an active parking facility required precision steel substructure design and safe installation around daily vehicle and pedestrian traffic, with cabling integrated cleanly through structural columns.

The Win

The completed solar carport shades hundreds of vehicles daily while generating substantial renewable energy. The client reduced grid dependency significantly and the structure became a visible statement of their sustainability commitment.

Start a Similar Project
Electrical Double-Volume Private Residence, Vaal Triangle

03.Luxury Chandelier Installation

The Brief

A newly built luxury home required installation of a large feature chandelier in a double-volume entrance hall. The client needed a team capable of working safely at extreme height while protecting brand-new interior finishes.

The Challenge

The double-volume height required full scaffolding inside a finished interior space, protecting marble floors, painted walls, and a curved staircase balustrade. The chandelier's weight and wiring required two electricians working simultaneously from different levels.

The Win

The chandelier was rigged, wired, and commissioned without a single scratch to the property's finishes. The installation transformed the entrance hall into the architectural showpiece the designer intended — delivered safely and on schedule.

Start a Similar Project
Solar & Electrical Multi-Site — Gauteng & Beyond

04.Large-Scale Commercial Hybrid Solar & Battery Systems

The Brief

Multiple commercial clients required serious hybrid solar and battery solutions capable of running entire operations through load shedding and grid failure — ranging from triple-inverter residential rooms to fully containerised industrial battery storage systems.

The Challenge

At this scale, complexity multiplies. Coordinating triple Sunsynk and Deye inverter installations, commissioning stacked battery banks in series, configuring automatic changeover, and ensuring all systems communicate correctly requires deep expertise across electrical and solar software.

The Win

FMD successfully commissioned multiple large hybrid installations including a fully containerised Sunsynk battery storage system with over 20 battery modules. Clients now operate with full energy independence — every installation COC certified and handed over with full documentation.

Start a Similar Project
Electrical Conference Facility & Industrial Client, Gauteng

05.Commercial Electrical Fit-Out & Control Panel Fabrication

The Brief

Two demanding commercial projects — a full recessed LED lighting installation across a large conference and events hall, and the design and fabrication of a custom automatic generator changeover control panel for an industrial client.

The Challenge

The lighting project required perfectly aligned rows across a suspended ceiling grid in a high-profile public venue. The control panel required custom fabrication: designing a mains/generator changeover system with automatic sensing, contactor switching, engine control terminals, and a 12V battery charger.

The Win

The events hall lighting was completed to architectural specification, transforming the venue's ceiling into a feature in its own right. The custom changeover panel was fabricated, tested, and delivered as a complete turn-key solution — protecting the client's business with fully automatic generator switching.

Start a Similar Project
